headline: Vote transfers between France's right and far right are increasingly common
subhead: By alternately voting for either the far right or the mainstream right to defeat the left, voters in the recent municipal elections have illustrated the two parties' ideological convergence.

headline: Trump insists Iran is negotiating with US, but 'they're afraid to say it'
Pierre Buhler, former diplomat: We are witnessing 'a worrying crack in the European edifice'
Cuba's escalating health crisis is 'deeply concerning,' says WHO chief
subhead: The World Health Organization's head described the 'immense turbulence' going on in Cuba, with 'energy shortages that have been affecting health.' Daily, up to 20-hour power outages affect parts of Cuba, which lacks fuel to generate power.

headline: Night train service resumes between Paris and Berlin
subhead: The Belgian-Dutch cooperative European Sleeper is reviving the symbolic rail link between the French and German capitals that was discontinued by SNCF in December 2025.

headline: The sensuality of Henri Rousseau's paintings at the Musée de l'Orangerie
subhead: The exhibition 'Henri Rousseau: A Painter's Ambition' offers a stimulating reinterpretation of the artist's work, highlighting his rigor and discipline, far removed from the cliché of the 'naïve' or 'Sunday painter.'

headline: Thierry Breton: 'A golden rule limiting the public deficit to 1% of GDP must be enshrined in the French Constitution by 2032'
subhead: A country that devotes an ever-increasing share of its resources to paying for its past is no longer in control of its destiny, says former finance minister Thierry Breton, while outlining his plan to put a stop to the rise in public debt.

subhead: Less than three months before the 2026 World Cup, the former French maestro is the clear frontrunner to succeed France's football squad head coach Didier Deschamps, who will step down at the end of the competition.
